event_blocks.summary: A woman with long dark hair, wearing a black blazer, introduces herself as the owner of two six-figure online businesses and welcomes viewers to her free 'STEP BY STEP Print on Demand Course,' contrasting its value with typical $1000+ courses. She encourages likes and subscriptions to support the creation of more free content, as a YouTube 'Subscribe' button animation appears. Quick cuts illustrate the course's practical applications: a person browsing an online store on a laptop, a delivery van unloading boxes symbolizing no inventory, and a boutique shopper. Text overlays declare 'THIS IS THE BEST FREE COURSE' and 'START WITH $0,' promising readiness to launch an online store with print on demand, suitable for anyone wanting to sell products online without holding inventory. An animated infographic titled 'How Print On Demand Works' details a three-step process: designing and selling a product online, customer ordering, and the print-on-demand company printing and shipping directly, with animated hands pointing to each step. This transitions to a five-step diagram showing the customer order, POD supplier involvement, printing with user artwork, packaging, and shipping, clarifying the user's role is design and product creation while the POD company handles the rest for profit. A 'Benefits' slide highlights starting with $0, no inventory, less saturation than dropshipping, fast shipping, and quick setup, reinforced by animated dollar bills and explanations of zero initial cost, no physical inventory, a less saturated market, and fast supplier shipping. The final 'WHAT YOU WILL LEARN' slide outlines topics like designing clothing, creating a logo, and building a POD store, with an icon symbolizing knowledge and profit, as the narrator begins listing course content.

Boutique shopper.description: A woman in a modern clothing boutique, browsing racks of clothes, serving as a visual example of the retail world Isabella's course aims to help people enter with zero upfront cost.

Person browsing laptop.description: A person sitting on a couch, using a laptop to browse an online clothing store, visually representing the target audience Isabella addresses in her narration about starting a clothing line or selling products online.

Woman with long dark hair.description: Isabella, a woman with long dark hair, wearing a black blazer, speaking directly to the camera against a blue background with animated money and tech graphics. She has a clear, enthusiastic, and persuasive voice, introducing herself as the owner of two six-figure online businesses and promoting her free print-on-demand course, detailing its benefits and how it works.

Animated hand.description: A white cartoon hand that appears in the infographic, pointing sequentially to each step in the print-on-demand process.

Animated infographic.description: A simple, colorful animated diagram explaining 'How Print On Demand Works' with three steps: designing and selling a product, customer ordering, and POD company printing/shipping. A white cartoon hand points sequentially to each step, while a female voice explains that the print-on-demand company will print and ship the product to the customer.

Delivery van.description: A white delivery van with its back door open, a worker unloading boxes, visually illustrating Isabella's point about avoiding the need to hold any inventory for an online business.

Five-step print-on-demand diagram.description: A detailed, numbered flowchart with teal arrows connecting five boxes, illustrating the customer placing an order, the order going to the POD supplier, the supplier printing the item with the user’s artwork, packaging it, and finally shipping it to the customer. A female voice elaborates on this process, stating that the user only needs to create the design, and the POD company handles all the rest, allowing the user to keep the profits.

Learning objectives slide.description: A light blue background with a list titled 'WHAT YOU WILL LEARN,' including topics like designing clothing, creating a logo, building a POD store, automating orders, and marketing. Accompanied by an icon of a lightbulb over a book and coins. A female voice introduces this as 'what you're going to learn in this free course' and begins to list the topics, including 'what print on demand really is, how to design your own clothing, how to create a logo.'

Benefits slide.description: A bright blue background with bold yellow text listing advantages of print-on-demand: starting with $0, no inventory commitment, less saturated than dropshipping, fast shipping, and starting in under 30 minutes. Animated green dollar bills appear on the right. A female voice directly explains each benefit, emphasizing the ability to start with zero dollars, no need for inventory (no 'boxes of clothes in your house'), less saturation compared to dropshipping, and the availability of suppliers with 'super fast shipping.'

event_blocks.summary: The video begins by presenting a 'Learning Objectives' slide detailing how to build a print-on-demand store for free, emphasizing marketing and order fulfillment, with visual cues like a lightbulb icon and 'FOR FREE $0 MONTHLY' text. A subsequent slide, 'HOW POD IS USUALLY TAUGHT,' contrasts this by crossing out the Shopify logo and a money-holding cat, as the speaker explains that traditional methods often involve unnecessary costs like Shopify subscriptions and ad buys, especially for beginners. She asserts her approach allows starting with $0, drawing from her own experience. The scene then shifts to a physical demonstration: hands place a UPS SurePost shipping box with a custom 'MEEKA THE HUSKY' label onto a wooden floor, showcasing 'Mika's merch store' and highlighting the ability to customize labels with company names and images. Inside the box, a packing slip is revealed, which, despite listing items like a white hoodie and t-shirts, omits original prices and instead includes a personalized thank-you message and Instagram promotion. The speaker praises the neat bagging and folding of the apparel. A white t-shirt with a vibrant husky graphic, 'FLUFF YOU!' and 'MEEKA' text is then displayed, with the speaker detailing how she created the design from an iPhone photo and uploaded it to Printful, commending the excellent print quality and reinforcing the course's focus on creating similar custom apparel.

Learning objectives slide.description: A light blue background with a list titled 'WHAT YOU WILL LEARN,' including topics like designing clothing, creating a logo, building a POD store, automating orders, and marketing, all explicitly detailed by a female speaker. Accompanied by an icon of a lightbulb over a book and coins, with additional text overlays 'FOR FREE $0 MONTHLY' and '& SO MUCH MORE!' appearing sequentially, reinforcing the speaker's emphasis on the course's cost-free nature.

Shipping box with custom label.description: A cardboard UPS SurePost shipping box on a wooden floor, featuring a custom label with a dog silhouette, the name 'MEEKA THE HUSKY,' and a barcode, with text overlay 'THEY ALLOW YOU TO CUSTOMIZE SO MUCH!' and 'WHAT YOUR CUSTOMER WILL RECEIVE.' A female voice highlights its extensive customization options, noting it appears to be from 'you' rather than Printful, and can include a company name and chosen picture.

Benefits slide.description: A bright blue background with the title 'HOW POD IS USUALLY TAUGHT:' featuring the Shopify logo and a cartoon cat holding money, both crossed out with red X's. This visually reinforces the female speaker's explanation that traditional POD methods involving Shopify and paid ads are unnecessary, especially for beginners. The bold text 'I WILL SHOW YOU HOW TO START COMPLETELY WITH $0' is highlighted, aligning with the speaker's personal anecdote of starting her own entrepreneurial journey without any initial capital.

Animated infographic.description: A simple, colorful animated diagram explaining 'How Print On Demand Works' with three steps: designing and selling a product, customer ordering, and POD company printing/shipping. Includes icons of a store, a customer, and a delivery truck, with animated hands pointing to each step, though not visible in this segment.

Packing slip.description: A printed packing slip listing purchased items (white hoodie, black crop top, men’s t-shirt) with a personalized thank-you message and Instagram promotion, held by a hand over the open box. A female voice clarifies that original prices are *not* on this slip, but it displays what was ordered, the customer's company name with a random address, and their chosen picture.

Custom t-shirt with husky graphic.description: A white t-shirt with a large, detailed graphic of a husky dog wearing a pink bandana with 'MEEKA' and the text 'FLUFF YOU!' above it, laid flat on a wooden surface. A female voice enthusiastically praises its 'nicely' printed design, explaining she uploaded a file, took the original picture with her iPhone, and added the words using Printful. The hand points to design details and the 'COTTON HERITAGE' tag inside the collar.

Hand interacting with box and clothing.description: A human hand with light skin tone, visible throughout the video, opening the box, holding the packing slip, removing and displaying the t-shirts, and pointing to design elements on the white t-shirt.

raw_segments: The video begins with a bright, light blue animated slide titled 'WHAT YOU WILL LEARN,' listing key topics such as 'WHAT IS PRINT ON DEMAND,' 'HOW TO DESIGN CLOTHING,' 'HOW TO CREATE A LOGO,' 'HOW TO BUILD YOUR POD STORE,' 'HOW TO FULFILL ORDERS & AUTOMATE ORDERS,' and 'HOW TO MARKET & GET SALES.' A female speaker articulates these points, emphasizing how to build a "print on demand store for free, of course, $0 monthly," and how to "market and get sales," concluding with how to "fulfill orders and connect the print on demand supplier with your online store." An icon of a lightbulb over a book and coins, along with text overlays 'FOR FREE $0 MONTHLY' and '& SO MUCH MORE!', visually reinforces the speaker's message about the course's cost-free nature. The scene then transitions to a new slide titled 'HOW POD IS USUALLY TAUGHT:' featuring the Shopify logo and a cartoon cat holding money, both crossed out with red X's. The speaker explains that "usually people that teach print on demand, they just always recommend the same thing, which is using Shopify and buying ads," but she finds "both of these things are unnecessary, especially for beginners." Bold text 'I WILL SHOW YOU HOW TO START COMPLETELY WITH $0' appears, aligning with her personal experience: "I definitely did not have any money when I first started my entrepreneurship journey." The video then shifts to a real-world setting, showing a pair of hands placing a cardboard UPS SurePost shipping box on a wooden floor. The speaker states, "So this is what your customer is going to receive," explaining she ordered samples. The box features a custom label with a dog silhouette and the name 'MEEKA THE HUSKY,' which the speaker identifies as "Mika's merch store." A finger points to the label, and text 'WHAT YOUR CUSTOMER WILL RECEIVE' and 'THEY ALLOW YOU TO CUSTOMIZE SO MUCH!' appear, as the speaker highlights how they "even put your company's name here" and "a picture that they customize on the label."

The video opens with a close-up of a cardboard UPS SurePost shipping box, featuring a custom label with a dog silhouette and the name 'MEEKA THE HUSKY.' A female voice immediately highlights the extensive customization options, noting that the label appears to be from 'you' rather than Printful, allowing for personalized branding with a company name and chosen picture. A hand then opens the box, revealing several items neatly wrapped in plastic. The female narrator explains that this is 'what we got' and pulls out a packing slip, clarifying that while it lists purchased merchandise like a white hoodie, black crop top, and a men’s t-shirt, it notably does *not* include original prices. Instead, it features a personalized thank-you message, Instagram promotion, and the customer's company name and chosen image. As the hand removes the items from the box, the narrator praises the packaging, noting that everything is neatly 'bagged' and 'folded,' not just 'thrown in here.' The hand then reveals a white t-shirt with a large, colorful graphic of a husky dog wearing a pink bandana with the text 'FLUFF YOU!' and 'MEEKA' beneath it. The t-shirt is laid flat on a wooden surface, and the female voice enthusiastically points out various design details, praising how 'nicely' the design printed. She explains that she uploaded a file, having originally taken the husky's picture with her iPhone, not a professional camera, and added the words using Printful, reiterating the excellent print quality. Text overlays appear, stating 'I WILL BE SHOWING YOU HOW YOU CAN CREATE CLOTHING LIKE THIS!', reinforcing the focus on the quality and design of the custom apparel and the personalized print-on-demand process.

event_blocks.summary: The video begins by showcasing custom apparel, starting with a white t-shirt featuring a husky graphic and the phrase 'FLUFF YOU!', identified as a size small from the brand 'COTTON HERITAGE'. A hand with pink nail polish points to the design. Next, a white hoodie with a similar husky graphic is presented, noted as being printed, not embroidered, with a 'super thick' material. The speaker, wearing a black hoodie with 'FAITH / FEAR' embroidered on the front, highlights the high quality of the embroidery and reveals 'Psalms 118:6' embroidered on the sleeve as a 'little reminder'. The tutorial then shifts to a screen recording of the Printful website, where the speaker introduces it as a favorite print-on-demand platform and guides viewers through creating an account using a channel-supporting link. After logging into the dashboard, the speaker navigates to 'Product templates' and demonstrates how to create a new product template, focusing on men's clothing and selecting a 'unisex hoodie'. The process moves to the 'Create a product template' interface, where the user selects a 'Men's Classic Tee | Gildan 5000' and chooses the 'Embroidery' technique. The speaker then guides the user through the 'Design' step, using the 'Add text' feature to create a 'FAITH over FEAR' design in white on the t-shirt, explaining font choices and customization options. The user selects the 'Antique Olive Nord' font, noting its masculine appearance, and resizes and repositions the text within the embroidery area, with a warning about staying within the designated design space. The speaker also mentions the option to center the design on the chest.

The user interacts with the 'Add text' feature, typing 'FAITH' and 'FEAR' into a text box, which appears on the shirt. The video opens with a screen recording of the Printful website homepage, where a female speaker introduces Printful.com as her "favorite platform for print-on-demand." A cursor navigates the site, clicking 'Sell with Printful' and then 'Platforms & marketplaces' as the speaker instructs viewers to create an account, mentioning a sign-up link in the description that supports her channel. The user then logs into their Printful dashboard, which the speaker identifies as "this page," greeted with a 'Welcome, Baddie in Business!' message and suggested products. The speaker then directs the user to 'Product templates' on the left, explaining that "this is where all of your product templates are going to be." A grid of existing templates is shown, and the speaker notes she has "a lot of different designs here" and will show how to replicate them. The scene transitions to a title card with bold text 'HOW TO CREATE PRODUCT DESIGNS FOR YOUR POD STORE,' featuring the Printful and Canva logos alongside an animated person designing on a computer, during which the audio is silent. The final frame displays a black hoodie with the text 'FAITH / FEAR' in white, labeled 'MADE IN PRINTFUL,' emphasizing the design's origin and quality, just as the speaker begins to speak again, saying, "So the first thing that I'm going to..."

The video segment opens with a screen recording of the Printful dashboard, specifically on the 'Product templates' page, where various existing templates like mugs and hoodies are displayed. A female voice explains the goal is to demonstrate how to make a hoodie, noting that the example is embroidered and 'really nice.' She then instructs the user to 'just click on product templates,' explaining that the interface will display 'all the different products that you can sell in your online store.' The user clicks the 'Create new' button, transitioning to the 'Create a product template' interface, which shows a grid of product categories. The voice confirms these categories, mentioning 'kids, accessories, home and living, like pillows and mugs,' and 'different branded items,' emphasizing the extensive customization options available. Following the voice's instruction to focus on 'men's clothing,' the user navigates through the categories, hovering over options like 'Men's clothing' and 'Hats' before selecting 'Men's clothing.' This leads to a subcategory view with options such as T-shirts, Hoodies, and Sweatpants & joggers, with the voice mentioning the intent to find a 'unisex hoodie' and defining it as 'for men or women.' The user then clicks on 'All shirts,' revealing a list of men's t-shirt products with details like price, reviews, estimated delivery, and available colors. The voice reiterates that 'they have so many different things that you guys can customize here.' The user hovers over several t-shirt options, including the 'Men's Classic Tee | Gildan 5000,' which is marked as a 'Bestseller' with a price of $10.95 and a 5-star rating. The voice identifies this as 'the men's classic tee' and explains its selection, stating it 'costs you around $11 to make it.' The segment ends as the voice highlights that 'the estimated delivery is 5 to 10 days.'

The video opens with a screen recording of the Printful website's 'Products / Men's / Shirts' section, displaying a grid of t-shirt options with models, prices, reviews, and delivery estimates. A female speaker explains that customer orders from the website will be delivered in 5 to 10 days. The user hovers over and clicks on the 'Men's Classic Tee | Gildan 5000', a bestseller priced at $10.95, as the speaker highlights the product's availability in "lots of different colors" for customization, aiding creative design. The interface transitions to the 'Create a product template' page, showing the selected maroon t-shirt with a 'Drop your design here' placeholder on the left chest. The speaker elaborates on the ability to drop a design, picture, or logo directly onto the shirt, or to create clothing with words or quotes. The user then interacts with the interface by hovering over the design area and selecting the 'Embroidery' technique from the 'Choose technique' options, leaving 'DTG printing' unselected. The speaker expresses enthusiasm for Printful's "embroidery option," noting it's "really nice" and can look "very expensive and nice." Following the speaker's instruction to "let's go ahead and click on design," the user clicks the 'Design' tab, revealing options such as 'Choose file', 'Add text', 'Add clipart', and 'Add Quick Design' for further customization. The clean, white interface with clear labels and interactive elements emphasizes the ease of product customization.

The video opens with a screen recording of the Printful website's 'Create a product template' interface, specifically in the 'Design' step. A female voice guides the viewer, explaining how to use the 'Add text' feature for a brand with quotes. A distinct clicking sound accompanies the user's action of clicking 'Add text' to begin customizing a maroon t-shirt. The speaker then states her intention to write 'FAITH', and typing sounds are heard as the word appears in white on the t-shirt's left chest. She mentions changing the color via 'Color and details' to white, noting it's for a 'black shirt,' despite the visual showing a maroon one. After another clicking sound for 'enter' and a line creation sound, the user types 'FEAR' below 'FAITH' (more typing sounds), creating a 'faith over fear' design. The speaker clarifies this is an example, not a logo. Finally, a clicking sound is heard as the user navigates back to the 'Font' option, preparing to adjust the font to 'Antique Olive Norm' from the selection menu, with the speaker highlighting the numerous font choices available. Throughout, the interface displays various design tools like 'Color & details', 'Font', 'Transform', 'Position', and 'Outline', as the text is modified and positioned in real-time.

The video opens on a clean, white Printful website interface, specifically the 'Create a product template' section, where a user is customizing a maroon t-shirt. The user adds text, initially displaying 'FAITH' and 'FEAR' in white, separated by a horizontal line, to the left chest area. A female voice explains the availability of "many different fonts here," noting how "you can make it look different" based on preference. As the user navigates the font selection menu on the left, scrolling through options like 'Antique Olive Nord' and 'Avenir Next Medium' before selecting 'Antique Olive Nord', the speaker comments that "this kind of font looks more masculine," suggesting it might be "better for this kind of shirt." The interface displays tools for text size, line height, letter spacing, alignment, color, transform, position, and outline. The user then resizes and repositions the text using drag-and-drop controls, adjusting its size and placement to fit within the designated embroidery area. The speaker emphasizes that "you can make it as big as you want, as long as it stays in this section," warning that if "you try to drag your design out of the design section, it won't let you do that." A red warning message briefly appears, reminding the user to keep the design within the embroidery area. The speaker also mentions an option to "put your design right in the middle," instructing to "just click on center chest," but clarifies that one must "pick either left chest or center chest," as "you can't have both."

event_blocks.summary: The Printful website interface, presented as a screen recording, shows a user designing a maroon t-shirt within the 'Create a product template' section. A female narrator guides the process, initially discussing the aesthetic choice of placing 'FAITH — FEAR' text on the left chest versus a more visible center chest placement for searchability. The user then modifies the text's font to 'Antique Olive Norm' and interacts with various design tools including 'Color & details', 'Font', 'Transform', 'Position', and 'Outline', with the narrator emphasizing the wide array of placement options like 'Large center', 'Left sleeve top', and 'Right sleeve top'. The focus shifts to the 'Inside label' placement, where the narrator stresses its branding significance. The user selects a pre-made label template containing 'SIZE', 'Made in Honduras', '100% cotton', and care symbols, but a 'Inside label template design can't be empty' warning appears, necessitating further customization. The narrator explains that adding such custom elements, like a personalized label, incurs an extra $2.50 charge, dependent on the user's prioritization of branded labels. The interface maintains a clean, white aesthetic with the t-shirt prominently featured on the right.

event_blocks.summary: The Printful website interface is demonstrated, beginning with a maroon t-shirt in the 'Create a product template' section where an initial warning pop-up is dismissed. A female narrator guides the user through customizing the t-shirt, explaining the various options available. The user selects 'Left chest' placement and adds the white text 'FAITH — FEAR', positioning it on the upper left. The narrator points out the 'clip art section' with numerous graphics, suggesting themes like 'saving animals' or 'non-profit'. The user then adds a small floral design labeled 'Caléndulas' from the 'Underwater kingdom' category, which the narrator notes can also be embroidered. The user places the clipart next to the text, adjusts its position, and sets the embroidery thread colors to '1848 Kiwi Green' and '1987 Orange'. After the narrator instructs to 'click continue when you're done with your design', the user moves to the 'Review' page, titling the product template 'Men's classic tee' and confirming details like product type (Embroidery), thread colors, and sizes. Subsequently, the user names the template 'Faith over Fear Unisex Tee' with distinct typing sounds, and the narrator clarifies that the 'men's' shirt is unisex. The user adds all available sizes from S through 5XL, explaining that Printful offers them. After saving the template, the 'Product templates' dashboard displays the new 'Faith over Fear Unisex Tee' template. The scene then transitions to a product page for a 'Unisex Faith over Fear Shirt' priced at $19.95, showing a maroon t-shirt with a 'FAITH / FEAR' design and floral elements, where the user selects size 'S' and color 'Pink', then clicks 'Add to Bag'. The video concludes with a title card introducing an 'ALTERNATIVE PRINT ON DEMAND METHOD: Etsy', featuring the Canva and Printful logos alongside an animated character typing on a computer, as the speaker begins to transition to the next topic.

The video opens with a static title card, 'ALTERNATIVE PRINT ON DEMAND METHOD: Etsy,' displayed in bold yellow and orange text against a light blue background. The Canva and Printful logos are on the left, while an animated character with dark hair, wearing a blue and purple long-sleeve shirt, is shown sitting at a desk and typing on a black computer keyboard on the right. A female voice immediately begins discussing this method, stating it's "another popular way that people do print on demand" using "Etsy, Canva, and Printful," with Etsy being where products are actually sold. She clarifies that she doesn't teach it this way and it's not her "favorite way" because "Etsy takes so many fees" even before money is made. She reminds viewers she's "all about starting with zero dollars" and offers to make a dedicated course on Etsy print on demand if requested in the comments, acknowledging it's a popular method many teach. She emphasizes that if you're marketing your own products, you should keep all the profit, but if you create an Etsy store and "they're getting you sales, then that's when it's worth it." The scene then transitions to a new title card reading 'HOW TO GET SALES WITH YOUR POD STORE WITHOUT PAYING FOR ADS!' in bold yellow and white. This card features a real-life video inset of a person's hands typing on a silver laptop, browsing an online clothing store with various t-shirt designs, while a cartoon stack of green money with wings appears on the right, symbolizing free sales. The speaker continues, asking, "Now that you have your print on demand store all set up, how are we going to get sales?" She explains, "This is going to be through the way that you promote and market your online store," and begins to introduce "one of the best ways to promote your print on demand store is by using Pinterest."

The video begins with a screen recording of the Pinterest mobile app, where a female voice explains that Pinterest launched "Idea Pins" allowing users to put a link directly into the pin, emphasizing the benefit of promoting through photos. She highlights Pinterest's function as a search engine, where users actively look for content. The screen displays a search for 'graphic tees' with various product pins, including a 'Main Character Energy Oversized Graphic Tee' from Miami Outfits. The user taps on this product, revealing a detailed page with a $45.00 price, white color, size L, and a 'Buy with Shop Pay' button. After navigating back to the Pinterest feed, showing fashion-related pins, the female voice transitions to demonstrating how to post an Idea Pin, mentioning the use of a free business account. The video then shifts to the Pinterest 'Create' interface, where the user, guided by the voice, clicks the plus button and selects 'Idea Pin'. A mock-up photo of a t-shirt featuring a husky graphic with 'FLUFF YOU!' text is chosen. The voice explains that the title section is for keywords, demonstrating by adding 'Husky lovers dog animal' as a title, clarifying it's for a "funny husky shirt" related to the "dogs" theme. On-screen text overlays like 'METHOD #1' and 'HOW TO GET SALES WITH YOUR POD STORE' appear against a light blue background with animated dollar bill icons, reinforcing the instructional tone. The user then moves to the 'Finishing touches' screen, where the voice begins to explain adding details in the notes section, as options for adding a link, picking a board, scheduling, tagging topics, and advanced settings are visible, all accompanied by upbeat, light electronic background music.

The segment opens with a screen recording of the Pinterest mobile app's Idea Pin creation interface, where a user types 'Husky lovers dog animal' into the title field. A female speaker explains that the 'details' section serves as the caption for the image, suggesting phrases like 'shop with us now,' before instructing viewers to add their shop link in the designated field. The user then navigates to 'Tag related topics,' types 'Dogs,' and selects multiple related tags such as 'Cute Dogs,' 'Funny Dogs,' 'Big Dogs,' and 'Small Dogs,' as the speaker emphasizes the ability to add numerous niche-related tags. After selecting five tags, the user returns to the main creation screen and clicks the 'Publish' button, highlighted by a purple arrow, following the speaker's instruction. The scene transitions to a Printful challenges web interface, displaying tasks like 'Full shelves' and 'International seller,' each with point values. The speaker explains that Printful offers an incentive program where completing tasks earns points to get samples of clothing. The user attempts to claim perks like 'Extra Sample Order' but receives 'Not enough points' notifications, reinforcing the speaker's advice to use this program if one cannot afford samples. The video then cuts to a bright blue background with bold yellow text reading 'HOW TO GET SALES WITHOUT BUYING ADS!', featuring the TikTok and Instagram logos. The speaker states that her favorite marketing methods include TikTok and Instagram Reels, as well as Pinterest. Below the logos, 'PEOPLE LOVE VIDEO CONTENT' appears, with animated dollar bill icons in the background, as the speaker elaborates on using video platforms for print-on-demand, noting that photos are the default unless samples are ordered to create videos.
